title: Rust Meshtastic bridge flips U/L bit vs canonical IID
description: rust/lichen-meshtastic/src/address.rs:193-199 (iid_to_link_local) and 162-165 (ipv6_to_meshtastic). Canonical LICHEN derivation (rust/lichen-link/src/identity.rs:21-27, python identity.py:118-130): IID = SHA-256(pubkey)[..8] with U/L bit cleared, embedded into addresses AS-IS (python node.py:191-194,593). address.rs derives the same IID then flips ^0x02 when forming the address, and flips again before map lookup — for the same pubkey the Rust bridge computes fe80::(IID|0x02...) while the Python node's real address is fe80::(IID&~0x02...). Latent (no external callers yet). Fix: VERIFY the canonical address-formation rule repo-wide; if embed-as-is is canonical, drop the ^0x02 in both functions.
